GS.rationale: Strong margins and stable revenue growth support the fundamental case, but a +53.3% one-year run and PE near the top of its 1y range (16.61x vs 11.4–18.3x) leave limited risk-adjusted upside.

Sequential revenue growth: 21.89B → 23.74B → 23.99B across 2025 quarters
FDA approval for precision prostate cancer combo on Dec 15 with BofA target hike
Stock already up 44.4% over 1 year and 11.3% over 3 months, limiting remaining upside

PG.rationale: Valuation compression to the low end of its recent PE range (22.5x) combined with solid 21% EPS growth and 29.7% ROE makes the risk-reward mildly attractive after a 14% one-year decline, though modest revenue growth (~3%) limits upside.

VZ.rationale: Valuation compression to the low end of its PE range plus improving fundamentals and a dividend-supported floor make VZ a mildly attractive risk-adjusted hold, though satellite competition and modest growth limit conviction.

CRM.rationale: Valuation has compressed to near the low end of its one-year range after a 21% drawdown, while sequential EPS growth is accelerating and balance-sheet risk is low — the reward/risk skews modestly positive but revenue growth remains tepid and part of the recovery is already priced in.

NKE.rationale: Nike trades at 37x depressed earnings with margins far below historical norms, but insider buying, an Apple partnership catalyst, and sequential revenue improvement suggest the turnaround is gaining traction — the asymmetric recovery potential outweighs the risk of further margin deterioration.

Insider buying and Apple/Tim Cook partnership news (Dec 24–29) provide confidence signals; sentiment improving from deeply negative (-1.00 on Dec 19) to positive (+1.00 on Dec 25 and Dec 29)

MMM.rationale: Revenue is recovering sequentially and valuation is reasonable at 20x, but high leverage (D/E 2.98) and a depleted equity base create outsized downside risk that offsets the modest upside from improving fundamentals.
